extraction.one_line_summary: VALGREN Michael won Amstel Gold 2018 by waiting until final 300m after Astana's two-man advantage forced rivals to stare rather than chase
tactical_patterns: Astana's two-rider advantage (Valgren/unnamed teammate) in final selection paralyzed chase dynamics, forcing rivals into tactical stalemate
Cauberg eliminated with 43.5km remaining, forcing selection on earlier Keutenberg climb rather than traditional finishing venue
GASPAROTTO caught final group from behind during hesitation phase, proving chase groups remained viable until 700m mark

rider_signals.VALGREN Michael: positive: Won despite course change removing traditional Cauberg finish that typically favors pure puncheurs
rider_signals.GASPAROTTO Enrico: positive: Third place after bridging from chase group shows exceptional late-race form despite being caught earlier
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Fourth despite being in winning move, still winless at Amstel revealing tactical weakness in multi-rider finales
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: Ninth place as American on parcours traditionally dominated by Benelux puncheurs signals climbing adaptation
